candidates.stmt:
I am a sitting Sandwell councillor and very proud of my record in Bearwood. I was born in America (in Hollywood!), but I am also British and have lived in the UK for over 20 years. The people of Bearwood are the most warm and welcoming - I'm so happy in the West Midlands, and I can't imagine wanting to live anywhere else.

I came to politics in mid-life as a single parent on benefits for the first time after being made redundant from my job. I found so many obstacles in my path, that didn't seemed pointless and unnecessary. It angered me. I thought about it and decided if I wanted to make my community better, I should stop commenting about the state of things and do something about it.

I've been fortunate in recent years and worked hard. I'm now in a better place, but I know so many others who are struggling . I want to make a difference for others in my community, be it on individual levels, throughout Bearwood, and across Sandwell.

As a married father of 2 children family values honesty, respect and trust mean everything to me.

I would also expect the same from my local authority, unfortunately this is quite often not the case.

As a Green Party candidate I would like the opportunity to ask the awkward questions that all to often don't get asked by the elected councillors for fear of losing their perks.

The Green Party offers a fresh approach to councils something we haven't seen for a long while HONESTY, RESPECT, and TRUST.

I am a local resident of Bearwood and have lived there my whole life. I have been an activist in the Labour Party for 7 years and have decided that a younger voice is needed to bring some change to our community and fresh ideas to create growth. 
I am a leader in a local youth group and cherish my time there as I believe that teaching community values at a young age is a priority, this belief is with me due to my own time in youth groups which taught me a lot of my morals and life skills. 
I have put myself forward as a candidate because I believe that my home deserves to be a place of pride. I want to be proud of where I live and ensure that the people there are looked after, especially in times such as now where everyone is struggling to make ends meet. Everyone deserves to have a safe and joyful community.

My name is Randall, I'm a British born citizen who's also a former NHS worker as well as a survivor of a migrant attack. The United Kingdom is a Christian nation made up of England, Scotland, Wales & Ireland & I intend to fight for the right of those born here and to put the country first. I care about the safety of women, children & the innocent and their rights. I also care about the rights of veterans and pensioners as well as the right to free speech. It's not racist to put your country first. I will fight for what's right.

Welcome, my name is Ray Nock and I’m proud to have been selected by Reform UK as the Prospective Parliamentary Candidate for West Bromwich in the next General Election.

I was born and raised in West Bromwich and worked in Manufacturing, Management and Education, I have been involved in local politics for over 30 years, and served as a councillor in the borough of Sandwell.

I simply could not sit by a moment longer, watching this government’s policies systematically ruin our Economy, Education, NHS, Law and Order, Housing and Transport.

Today I’m proud to represent a party who will help those struggling with rapidly rising living costs, and with wages and pensions that buy less and less, seemingly every week.

We have excellent plans to bring back well paid jobs and boost local trade, plans which will get our economy moving again, and return our towns to being safe, vibrant and prosperous. 

If anyone would like to know more I can be contacted on the phone number or email address above.

Amrita has been raised in Sandwell and wants to give back to the community that she has been brought up in for the last two decades. Living near Tividale ward, Amrita shares the same daily struggles as the residents she wishes to represent. Amrita wants to continue to build on the strong efforts of the local Conservative team to improve the area for everyone.

In particular, Amrita plans to protect and secure the future of our community in Tividale, tackling major issues like Houses of Multiple Occupancy, speeding and protecting our green spaces.

Amrita recognises the importance of issues such as road safety, anti – social behaviour and the preservation of green spaces. Therefore, Amrita is working closely with Shaun Bailey MP to continue his work, and make sure that local residents are heard and ensure their interests are put first.

Amrita says, ‘Sandwell has so much potential. I believe with hardworking, enthusiastic, and concerned individuals, we can make the difference that the people of Sandwell rightfully deserve.’.

Being a Young Conservative, Amrita has an active and dynamic yet pragmatic approach to situations and hopes to use her high – spirited nature to make sure that there are real and proper answers to any concern raised by local people.

I’ve worked most of my life in retail Sales, I was a manager in a large superstore, progressing to become manager of a large international supermarket store.

Since 2003 I’ve owned a convenience store in West Bromwich with my wife. I’m also a fully qualified Driving instructor and was involved in Local Politics for over 20 years. For years I've donated to local charities, I now feel I could help everyone with Reform UK. I know how things are.

I have experienced at first hand the severe problems our retail businesses, towns, markets & SMEs are currently going through, with high energy and running costs, and continuing extremely damaging food price inflation hurting everyone.

I’ve seen the hardships brought onto people by Conservative & Labour governments. It has to stop. We in Reform UK have the plans to bring back wealth and good paid jobs, and to lower your energy costs.

With your help, we can do it!

As an Independent candidate for West Bromwich East it is my mission to improve the lives of the whole community with a caring leadership style.  As a Christian I would like to apply the values of working together with people of all backgrounds, helping society’s most vulnerable and meeting the daily needs that are vital for the function and success of the area.

I feel it is my duty to serve the area where I was born and raised.  I would like to thank all of my supporters throughout this election process and look forward to connecting with more members of the community.

To date, I have worked closely with residents within a number of capacities and I am confident that I can be that voice to bring the concerns of West Bromwich East to Parliament in order to make a long lasting and effective change through strong leadership. 

Some of the issues that I would like to tackle:
Crime
I would like to support the agenda to reduce the level of crime within West Bromwich East by providing services to boost aspirations and opportunities.  
I also plan to introduce an effective anti-corruption task force.  This is to replace ineffective complaints processes in order to root out corruption within industries which exploit society's most vulnerable people.  This will make a fairer society for all people. 
I am aware of antisocial behaviour that occurs out of boredom.  I want to actually address THE WHY by creating practical solutions and opportunities for growth and development that young people can actually enjoy.   
As the MP for West Bromwich East, I will EFFECTIVELY UTILISE current community buildings and land to create functions catered to meet the needs of the community as a whole.  I will encourage a culture of community engagement which will lead to a more stabalised society where young people can access much needed guidance through safe, regular schemes and programmes.      
Programmes will focus of positive outcomes such as life skills and basic academic tra

As the youngest candidate, William embodies energy and vitality coupled with maturity and realism. His youth means he is aware of the needs and aspirations of young people and is concerned to build a better future for everyone in Sandwell. His realism means that he knows that a lot of people in Great Bridge feel neglected and taken for granted by the present council administration. William has fresh ideas and a determination to reverse that feeling. His aim is to build a local community in which everyone has a stake and in which all can take pride. Raised in Sandwell, William lives in Great Bridge so knows the area and its problems. He was head boy at the Q3 Academy in Tipton. Already he has a record of community service having worked with Black Country Womens’ Aid and at the local food bank. His concern for humanity and opposition to injustice led to his involvement with the Holocaust Education Trust as part of the Belsen75 project.

Hi, my name is Pete Durnell, and I’ve lived in what is now Langley ward, for more than 30 years.

The first 20 or so of these, I worked in a variety of IT roles, essentially a “problem fixer”, for the last ten years I’ve been heavily involved with politics,

- decades of useless and self-serving Conservative and Labour administrations, both locally and nationally, have left our country with a huge number of very serious problems which need fixing urgently.

I have witnessed our local towns transformed, from thriving to threadbare, from prosperous to poverty stricken, from being a great place to work and spend leisure time, to “best avoided”

After 55 years of Labour rule, there are unfortunately a lot of problems to fix in Sandwell.

I helped set up Reform UK in the West Midlands five and a half years ago, because I believed then that a new party, with common sense policies and made up of committed, passionate people, who care deeply about the UK,

- was the only hope of turning the fortunes of our local area, and great country around.

I still believe it, and am proud to be still playing my part.

We need politicians driven by common sense, passion, and an unwavering desire to focus on doing what’s best for our country and everyone who lives here.

Not just themselves.

Politicians who are approachable, willing to listen, and if elected, will be fully committed to helping the people they serve.

If I am elected in Langley, where I live, I will hold monthly surgeries and always do everything I can to help those who live in the local area.

Conservatives and Labour have sadly, completely and utterly “broken Britain”, but we can, with your help

- now begin the task of making Sandwell a great place to live and work again.

The opinion polls are clear, only voting Reform will release Sandwell from Labour's disastrous grip on Thursday May 7th,

- so I would be grateful if you would lend me your vote.

Thank you!

I feel incredibly privileged to represent the Labour Party as your parliamentary candidate for Droitwich and Evesham.

I am currently a research and policy manager for a local healthcare organisation. My role is focused on ensuring that decisions and changes to health and social care services in Birmingham are totally focused on public need – this involves listening to people to ensure that their views come first especially those who are not easily heard. Including championing what matters to people and working with others to find ideas that work for people. It also involves empowering people to demand services and giving them agency to engage with those that provide and commission services. I hope to bring this way of working into my role as Labour MP for Droitwich and Evesham. I will work hard to understand your challenges, concerns, and priorities, and work tirelessly to champion these when decisions are made.

I have spent the last few weeks meeting local people in Droitwich & Evesham, like yourself, so I can understand the issues that matter to you. You have told me that you are concerned that our high streets are ghost towns and concerns with anti-social behaviour. You can see that the NHS is no longer delivering for us and there is a lack of a lasting solution to immigration. The people of Droitwich and Evesham bear the burden of the cost-of-living crisis necessitating foodbanks in our area. Sewage is polluting our rivers. The affordable houses needed are not being built. Our public transport is lacking, and we struggle with traffic and congestion. We need a strategy that improves the connectedness of our villages so people don’t feel forgotten or excluded.

Things were not always like this; the conservative government has let us down. Its time to rediscover our ambition, bring hope and usher in the change that is needed for Droitwich and Evesham. The Labour party has set out its mission to get our future back, focusing on economic stability, getting our NHS

I’m not a politician. I’m a project manager. I focus on getting things done.
I won’t hide in the council offices. I’ll knock on every door in Oldbury, asking you what you want me to do. Then I will do it, to the best of my ability. 
I’ll work with anyone who wants to fix our problems. I don’t care if an idea comes from the left or the right — if it’s a good idea, I’ll back it.

Welcome to my page, my name is Gary Dale, and I’m proud to be Reform UK’s Parliamentary Candidate for the constituency of Kingswinford and South Staffordshire.

I’ve spent most of my life in Manufacturing, Engineering & Business and have been involved in politics for over 5 years.

Why I am standing for Reform UK in Kingswinford and South Staffs?

Because I believe that:

“ENOUGH IS ENOUGH”

“We must take back control of our borders, return migrants attempt to cross the Channel illegally to France, and end all forms of illegal immigration.

“Being unable to see a doctors or dentist for weeks” – is simply not acceptable.

“What is happening to our Town Centres & SMEs “– is not acceptable.

“Out of control anti-social behaviour that has not been addressed, with no authority presence seen on our local streets” – is not acceptable.

“Sky high energy and living costs, continuing and extremely damaging levels of inflation”, a crazy rush to get to Net Zero with no regard for the jobs it will cost”

- just not acceptable.

Our farms are being put under massive political pressure to produce less food, to appease climate change alarmists, meaning we have to import more food – not acceptable.

I’ve seen at first hand the severe problems every UK business faces.

With no small businesses, we will have no economy, we must

Reinvigorate the British economy and making work pay by cutting regulations and taxes Slash energy prices and reawaken British industry by making use of our own vast energy resources Thank you.

I’m a working class kid done good, but it wasn’t a straight line. I spent years working admin jobs and call centre work, topped up with Universal Credit and sometimes sleeping on friend’s sofas and in colleague’s spare rooms. What I learnt there was that the link between hard work and a dignified life has been brutally severed. Liz Truss and Rishi Sunak have made that more and more true. There are too many people slogging their guts out to stand still.    I was one of them, but I got lucky. I got a break. I had to graft to get ahead but I was able to do it, because of good people who held their hands out to help me. I am in politics because I want to give people back the chance to work their way to a good and dignified life for themselves and their families. I don’t just want to hold my hand out, I want to close the gap so that nobody has to.    That’s not something that I can do by myself, nobody can. I remember when I was a kid, on the council estate with concrete roads in inner-city Birmingham where I was born, all of the neighbours coming round to help my Dad dig out a hedge. I remember Charlie, the brickie next door who kept racing pigeons, going round to the old lady’s across the street every week to put her bins out for her. I remember people helping each other. That’s why I’m in the Labour Party, the party that understands in our blood that by the strength of our common endeavour we achieve more than we can achieve alone. It is only by coming together, in our institutions of collective self-help – trade unions, co-operatives, local government and soon, we hope, Parliament and national government – that we can get Britain back on track.    For too long under the Conservatives Britain has been run down and let down. It’s time for a change. It’s time to vote Labour.

Les has lived in West Bromwich his whole life and knows the town inside out from his time living in Greets Green, Tantany and Lyndon. Charlemont is where Les and his young family now call home, after moving to the area in 2013. 

“If I’m lucky enough to be elected in May, myself and Cllr David Fisher can really start to make some big changes in Charlemont with Grove Vale. This is your chance to elect a serious and hardworking candidate who will always act in the best interests of the residents. I have a strong eye for detail and will ensure our ward is noticed at the highest level to gets the funds, treatment and opportunities it deserves.” 

Outside of politics, Les is a Director of a Sandwell based Health & Social Care company and a Director-Trustee of a West Bromwich based charity.

Aqeela's backround and experience  •Supporting young people and their families through roles within children's services. 

•Worked for the NHS - Helping prevent the spread of covid 19.

• A public governor for the Birmingham Women's and Children's hospital trust.

I'm a lifelong resident of Smethwick who has lived in St Paul's for over a decade. I want to put forward a message of optimism and hope for the future, to counter the divisive racist politics of Reform UK, and provide an alternative to the abject failure of Labour to meaningfully improve people's lives.

Labour has run Sandwell Council for over 50 years and is also currently in national government. Having been a Labour member for over 5 years until 2025, I could not be more disillusioned with what my former party has become. From refusing to tax the exorbitant wealth of the super rich, to attacks on migrants, pensioners, the disabled and LGBTQ+ people, and of course supporting the horrific genocide Israel is conducting in Gaza.

Starmer's party is not the Labour Party we once knew. This is why I'm standing as Socialist, progressive candidate for St Paul's, my home ward.

I am endorsed by the Climate Activist/Wealth Inequality group Green New Deal Rising, We Deserve Better, and Zarah Sultana alongside being a member of the community Union Acorn, as well as a signatory of the Vote Palestine pledge on behalf of the Palestine Solidarity Campaign.

In addition I am aligned with the left-wing, progressive politics represented by Jeremy Corbyn and Zarah Sultana.

A Tipton resident, to stand up for Tipton Town and work hard for my neighbours. You can choose three candidates this year, but, as the only Independent standing in this ward, you will always get the truth from me, with no party politics.  Whatever else you vote, make the INDEPENDENT one of your choices.

From Catwalk to Council 👠

For over twelve years I have used my platform as a beauty queen as a means of giving back by working with the press and a range of media outlets to shine a light on subjects that are too often left unspoken.

This has allowed me to help open important conversations around hidden disabilities, including mental health, sexual assault, the motherhood penalty, the rising cost of childcare, overcoming bereavement, the importance of men’s mental health, workplace discrimination and many other issues that I believe deserved greater public attention.

The confidence, discipline and eloquence I have developed through pageantry have shaped my approach to advocacy and inspired me to become a trailblazer for change.

Since becoming a charity Ambassador last year I have felt even more motivated to deepen my work within my community.

With opportunities like attending events at the House of Lords and the Houses of Parliament including last year’s  Disabled Women in Politics event as a Sandwell resident have truly opened my eyes to what can be acheived if I keep using my voice.

Despite navigating difficult circumstances as a solo parent and residing in a neglected area this opportunity isn’t only meaningful it is a movement!

Although I was born and raised in Bearwood the Tipton Green Ward has been my postcode for a while and needs serious support to get it back on track by removing politions who are too comfortable taking no action against challenges and experiences of this community.

With 3 weeks to go I’m elated to share I’m running as a candidate to become a councillor in the Tipton Green Ward and have been welcomed with open arms by all @sandwellgreenparty who are an ever growing community of likeminded people that are passionate about changing the lives of others in their wards.

I have lived or worked around the Blackheath area for decades, and my children grew up in this area. I have decades of experience in industry and the voluntary and public sectors, including a lot of community development work in Sandwell. I have been an educator for most of my career, and am currently a lecturer in public health at Birmingham City University. I have seen how local people have been let down by Sandwell Labour, and by right wing politicians. The local economy has been neglected, as have social conditions. The physical environment is also unhealthy - and only the Green Party is serious about putting the quality of life first. Vote for the Green Party, for a real, positive alternative.

Who I Am

My name is Tom Lewandowski and I live here in Tipton Green.

I'm not a career politician — and I never planned to be one. But after years of seeing our area being neglected by those meant to look after it, I decided it was time to step up instead of just complaining.

Why I'm Standing

Simply put — I got fed up.

Fed up with streets not being cleaned properly.
Fed up with problems being ignored.
Fed up with a council that seems more focused on its image than on getting things fixed.

Everyone can see things aren't working as they should.

Tipton Green deserves a councillor who lives here, understands the problems first-hand, and isn't afraid to speak up when something isn't good enough.

That's exactly what I aim to do.

What I Do

I work in the private sector, where results matter.

If you don't deliver, you don't last — and I believe the same standard should apply to public services.

I believe in practical solutions, not political waffle.
In getting things done, not explaining why they can't be.
And in treating every pound of taxpayers' money with care and respect.

My Connection to Tipton Green

This isn't just an area on a map to me — it's my home.

It's where I walk my dog, do my shopping, and know the people around me.

When I knock on doors, I'm not visiting — I'm speaking to neighbours.

The problems people raise are the same ones I see every day.

And that's exactly why I want to represent Tipton Green — to make sure those issues are properly heard and properly dealt with.

I am proud to be standing in the Wednesbury ward this year because I want to represent neighbours like you and me who aren’t seen or heard by our current councillors. Representation is still low for marginalised communities, and there is no one like me present.  Even in 2026 I question why.

I’m not standing for the title or for my career. I’m standing because I care. Our ward has never had an independent councillor before, and I would be proud to be the first.
I know many of you want change, and so do I. Some of you may be thinking, “Why should I vote for you when I don’t even know you?” That’s a fair point. I tried the political party route, but it became apparent that politics wasn’t accessible to people like me when it should be. Politics shouldn’t be about games or point‑scoring, it should be about people’s lives, especially ours.

As a South Asian disabled woman, I’ve always wanted to get into politics, but I’ve experienced many barriers. I know I’m not alone in that. These barriers aren’t about avoidance, they’re about a lack of accessibility. Part of the reason I’m standing is to improve representation, but also because I don’t want others to go through what I have. My motto has always been: “It doesn’t matter about me, what matters is that you don’t have to go through what I have.”

I’m not here to say I will fight XYZ. I’m here to say I’m by your side. If I’m elected, I may hold a title, but I want to use that title only to make our community and your life better.

I’m not your typical independent. I’m not here to say “don’t vote Reform” or “don’t vote Labour.” I’m not here to play the usual tactics that parties play, and I’m not going to speak at you like a politician. I’m here to speak as one human being to another. I’m ready to stand by your side, make sure you are heard in the chambers where change can happen, and put you and our community first.
